cause_analysis.narrative: Manu Royal Touch churned on 2026-04-16 after 130 days with Zoca. Conversation collapsed into a 23-day silence starting 2026-02-18. Classification: explicit cancellation. The client formally asked to cancel; the question is whether a save attempt was made. 2 inbound message(s) went unanswered for 5+ days — this is the clearest operational failure on Zoca's side.
recommendations: [Retention] Cancellation was signalled 3 day(s) before churn via sms. Trigger an AM save call within 4 hours of any cancellation keyword — not a support response.
[Offer] Build a 3-tier retention offer ladder (pause / discount / scope change) so AMs have something concrete to counter with.
[SLA] 2 inbound message(s) went unanswered for 5+ days. Enforce a hard rule: every inbound gets a human response within 24h, every day of the week.

[SLA example] On 2026-02-18 via email, client wrote: "Subject: Looking forward to connect\n\nBody: Hey Magnela,  I hope you are doing well. I'm reaching out regarding the pay…" — no outbound response in 23 days.

[SLA example] On 2026-02-18 via email, client wrote: "Subject: Looking forward to connect\n\nBody: Hey Magnela,  I hope you are doing well. I'm reaching out regarding the pay…" — no outbound response in 23 days.
